elements.doc: Minimal stand-in for the SysML v2 standard ScalarValues library. The nomograph indexer does not bundle the full standard library, so without this the primitive attribute types (String, Real, ...) resolve to nothing and show up as dangling references. Declaring them here keeps `nomograph-sysml check dangling-references` clean. Replace with the real standard library import if/when nomograph ships one.

Shared modeling vocabulary for the sysledge knowledge base.  Every system model (nanos3reader today; nanotins/nanolance/nanoarrow2parquet and future hardware systems later) imports this package so facts are recorded the same way everywhere. Keeping it small and stable is deliberate — see docs/conventions.md.
Pull a byte range [offset, offset+length) and seek within the object.
Consumer reads ranges; supplier serves them from a backing store.
Yields access key, secret key, optional session token + expiry.

Top-level entry point for the sysledge knowledge base — the first file an agent reads to discover what systems are modeled. Import a system's packages here as it is added.  Systems modeled:   - nanos3reader  (pilot)  — minimal read-only S3 range reader for C++ Planned (from GitLab): nanotins, nanolance, nanoarrow2parquet, then HW/embedded systems.

Physical/logical structure of nanos3reader, extracted from include/nanos3reader/s3_reader.h and src/s3_reader.cpp at nanos3reader@ff28f0b. Parts mirror the real C++ types; the crypto and TLS backends are modeled as variation points because they are genuine build-time variants.
Provides SHA-256 (and HMAC-SHA256 built generically on it) for SigV4.
Default: SHA-256 from OpenSSL libcrypto.
In-tree compact SHA-256 (FIPS 180-4); links no crypto library.
Resolved-once S3 configuration.
Thread-safe provider walking the credential chain, caching + refreshing temporaries.
Seekable std::streambuf over S3 range GETs; owns one reused libcurl handle and one window.
std::istream owning its S3Streambuf, returned to the caller.
Process-singleton factory: resolves Config once and opens S3 objects as seekable istreams.

Requirements for nanos3reader — a minimal, read-only S3 range reader for C++. Extracted from the repository at nanos3reader@ff28f0b (README.md, headers, sources). Every requirement carries @Provenance naming the source it was read from.
Open an s3://bucket/key object as a seekable, read-ahead-buffered std::istream.
Reads are served by HTTP(S) Range GETs (bytes=start-last), never whole-object fetches.
Each GET pulls a read-ahead window; slices within a window cost no extra GET.
One libcurl handle is reused across windows so the connection stays keep-alive per object.
Requests are signed with AWS Signature Version 4, validated against AWS test vectors.
Resolve credentials: env -> shared profile files (incl credential_process) -> ECS/EKS -> EC2 IMDSv2.
Temporary credentials are refreshed a few minutes before they expire.
On failure, the error names every credential source tried and why each yielded nothing.
AWS_ENDPOINT_URL selects path-style addressing for MinIO/R2/other S3-compatible stores.
A wrong-region response is retargeted once using the x-amz-bucket-region header.
Transient transport/5xx/429 failures retry with full-jitter exponential backoff; AWS_MAX_ATTEMPTS tunes it.
Depend only on libcurl + a small SHA-256 — no AWS SDK.
SHA-256 backend is chosen at build time (OpenSSL or bundled) with byte-identical signatures.
Support a small self-contained static binary (~2.2 MB with mbedTLS curl + bundled SHA-256).
Read/GET-only: no PUT, no multipart, no LIST, no SSO/assume-role resolution.

Verification cases for nanos3reader and the requirements each one verifies, extracted from the test suite and CI at nanos3reader@ff28f0b. "verify" links let an agent see what is actually proven by a test versus only asserted by code.
Checks sha256(""), derive_signing_key, and a get-vanilla Authorization against AWS vectors.

Exercises path-style addressing via AWS_ENDPOINT_URL, real range GETs, read-ahead window refetches, seeking within and across windows, EOF, and error reporting on a missing key — checked byte-for-byte against a local copy. Self-skips (exit 77) without IT env vars.
openssl and bundled SHA-256 both pass the SigV4 known-answer test => byte-identical signatures.

Traceability: which component satisfies which requirement. This is the "facts" backbone an advisory agent follows to answer "is requirement X actually implemented, and by what?". satisfy targets the requirement definitions directly (nomograph's completeness checks are definition-centric), binding each to the part that realizes it, all at nanos3reader@ff28f0b.
Behavior of nanos3reader's read path, extracted from src/s3_reader.cpp at nanos3reader@ff28f0b. Actions mirror the real control flow so agents can reason about it without re-reading the C++.

Walk the chain in order, returning the first source that yields usable keys:   1. env (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID/SECRET/SESSION_TOKEN)   2. shared profile files, then the profile's credential_process helper   3. ECS/EKS container endpoint (AWS_CONTAINER_CREDENTIALS_*)   4. EC2 instance role via IMDSv2 (falls back to IMDSv1) On total failure, accumulate a per-source diagnostic for error().
Construct the canonical request, string-to-sign, signing key, and Authorization header (SigV4).

Fetch [start, start+readAhead-1] into the window:   - sign + issue a Range GET on the reused curl handle (keep-alive)   - 200/206 -> install window; 416 -> empty window at EOF   - wrong-region (301/307/400 + x-amz-bucket-region) -> retarget once, retry free   - transient transport / 5xx / 429 -> full-jitter backoff, up to maxAttempts   - otherwise -> fail() sets the thread-local last error
Move the get pointer; if the target is inside the live window, reposition with no GET, else LoadWindow.
Parse s3://bucket/key, verify credentials resolve, construct an S3Streambuf wrapped in an S3IStream. Returns nullptr (with error() set) on a bad URI or missing credentials.
